item1.parentNote: May be deemed parent companies based on ownership

predecessors.name: BEC Longmont, Inc.
predecessors.business: operated coffee retail businesses, developed licensed methods

affiliates.name: High Grounds Coffee Equipment Sales and Service LLC

item1.businessDescription: Retail coffee shop selling gourmet specialty coffee, espresso, tea, fruit smoothies, dirty soda drinks, energy drinks, sandwiches, wraps, packaged coffee, and merchandise

multiUnitStructures.ninePak: typically 3 initial franchise agreements + Development Agreement for remaining 6

item5.launchFeeTimingMultiUnit: 10 days after signing lease/purchase agreement for that location

item6.royaltyPayment: weekly on Monday based on prior week

localAdvertising.note: Franchisor may later require local advertising spend

regionalAdvertising.note: May be established, may absorb part of marketing/tech fee or local ad spend

technologyAndMonitoringFees.name: Mystery shopper
technologyAndMonitoringFees.amount: $38/visit, at least monthly, more if scores missed

includes: all sales of products/services through shop
excludes: gift card/certificate sales (but franchisor may change this treatment)
grossSalesDefinition.note: Gift card accounting treatment subject to change on notice

otherFees.name: Management fee (default/abandonment)
otherFees.amount: 5% of Gross Sales plus direct costs

otherFees.name: Late payment interest
otherFees.amount: lesser of 1.5%/month or highest lawful rate

otherFees.name: Act of Deception audit charge
otherFees.note: Immediately upon notice if Act of Deception found

damagesFormula.formula: average weekly royalty from prior 52 weeks x weeks from termination to original expiration
damagesFormula.note: Effectively accelerates all future royalties on termination

notes.lowEndAssumption: Build-to-suit lease where landlord bears much of construction cost
notes.equipmentPackageIncludes: required POS equipment + espresso machine from High Grounds

affiliateSoleSupplier.name: High Grounds Coffee Equipment Sales and Service LLC

posSystem.hardware: Revel iPad POS System with Zebra drink-label printer

trainingProgram.location: operating Ziggi's store or training facility in Mead, Colorado
trainingProgram.onlineOption: some classroom content may be provided online

reportingRequirements.dataOwnership: franchisor claims ownership and broad use rights including for future FPR
reportingRequirements.auditScope: may extend to affiliated parties and owners

developmentAgreementTerritory.note: Under Development Agreement, franchisee does not receive exclusive territory. May face competition from other franchisees, franchisor-owned outlets, other channels, or competing brands franchisor controls.

item17.unresolvedNote: Full Item 17 chart not surfaced in this pass

damagesFormula.formula: average weekly royalty from prior 52 weeks x weeks from termination to original expiration

item17.spousalGuarantyNote: Special risks page confirms spousal liability; exact guaranty form not surfaced

metricTypes: Gross Profits (sales minus COGS and labor)

tableStructure.tablesAB: affiliate-owned shops that operated entire 2024 calendar year
tableStructure.tablesCD: franchisee-owned shops that operated entire 2024 calendar year
tableStructure.tableE: all shops (affiliate + franchisee) that operated at any time 2019-2024, including partial years

item19.dataVerification: reported by affiliates and franchisees, not audited or verified
item19.unresolvedNote: Full table A-E values not surfaced in this pass — affiliate data from prior pass preserved

churnExamples.California: 2 terminations in 2023, recovered with 2 openings in 2024
churnExamples.Wyoming: closed store but still has other opened locations — did not leave system

item20.systemNote: Growth driven almost entirely by franchise expansion. 209 unopened with agreements = large committed pipeline.

item21.financialConditionNote: Front-end risk disclosure says financial condition 'calls into question' ability to provide support
item21.pipelineNote: 301 units open or under development (90 franchisee-owned + 2 fee-waived + 209 unopened with agreements)
item21.financialStrengthNote: Audited but reporting net losses all 3 years and negative members equity. Revenue growing ($6.86M) but not yet profitable. Large pipeline may change economics.

formerFranchisees.transfersVsFailures: Several entries marked as transfers, not classic failed exits
formerFranchisees.wyomingNote: One entry closed store but still has other opened locations — did not leave system

universalRules: Multi-format coffee franchise needs separate investment objects per format (Drive Thru vs Café vs Endcap)
Multi-unit fee structures (3-Pak, 9-Pak) need parallel fee objects, not single scalar
Launch fee per franchise is separate from initial franchise fee — both must be captured
Affiliate sole supplier (High Grounds for espresso machines) is material and should be first-class
Minimum inventory requirement ($7,500 coffees) is both working-capital reality and contractual control point
Item 19 with separate affiliate and franchisee populations by store format needs structured table objects
Item 19 longitudinal outlet counts (2019-2024) tie FPR to system growth — preserve as metadata
Item 20 open units (100) vs open-or-under-development pipeline (301) are different but both buyer-relevant
Financial condition concern + audited financials showing net losses + large pipeline = preserve all three signals
Act of Deception audit charge ($25,000) is an unusually punitive enforcement mechanism
Damages formula (average weekly royalty x weeks to expiration) effectively accelerates all future royalties
Gift card accounting treatment 'subject to change on notice' is a revenue-base risk worth flagging
Development Agreement territory explicitly non-exclusive even if single-unit territory may differ — capture both layers
Former franchisee list distinguishes transfers from failures — not every exit is a system departure
